title: fix(core): restore stdout pipe inheritance for Windows LocalSandbox

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(core): fix Windows shell command spawning
similarOrDuplicatePRs.relationship: supersedes — fixes the exact same root cause (detached: true breaking stdout pipe inheritance on Windows) with the same approach (conditional detached, taskkill for process tree cleanup), but with a cleaner implementation using async execa for taskkill and proper platform-branched execaOptions

summary: Community PR fixing Windows LocalSandbox stdout pipe inheritance broken by `detached: true` in child_process.spawn(). Makes `detached` conditional on platform (!isWindows), adds a synchronous `killProcessTree()` helper using `taskkill /T /F /PID` on Windows, and updates the kill/timeout paths to use it. Also includes a changeset. However, the diff has a bug: `const isWindows` is declared twice, which would cause a runtime error.
maintainerNotes: This PR has been **superseded by #13886** which was merged on 2026-03-09. PR #13886 fixes the exact same root cause (detached: true breaking Windows stdout piping) with the same solution approach but a cleaner implementation: it uses async execa instead of execSync for taskkill, properly branches execaOptions per platform instead of a single `detached: !isWindows`, and doesn't have the duplicate `isWindows` declaration bug present in this PR. The maintainer (DanielSLew) originally asked for conflict resolution and changeset — the author did rebase and add a changeset, but in the meantime #13886 landed. This PR should be closed with a thank-you to the author for identifying the issue.
